surface.name: Harness CI first Cloud build pipeline
surface.selection_basis: Under the workshop policy, the primary route is Harness’s official CI onboarding quickstart: it is the recommended first build path, uses Harness-managed Cloud infrastructure, and reaches a smaller executable outcome than CD. Account creation is treated as one opaque step at the official signup URL; the public guide fully documents the post-login pipeline sequence.

alternatives_considered.name: Harness CD first deployment
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: A deployment adds service, environment, infrastructure, manifest, and target credentials beyond the CI guide's earlier executable outcome.

alternatives_considered.name: Remote pipeline storage
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: A Remote pipeline adds Git connector and repository-location setup. Inline is offered during creation and requires fewer dependencies.

alternatives_considered.name: Self-managed build infrastructure
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: The onboarding guide uses Cloud build infrastructure with default operating-system and architecture choices, while self-managed infrastructure adds installation and operation.

entry_point.developer_intent: Start without a Harness account, create or enter a Harness account, provide a source-code repository, create an inline CI pipeline, add a Build stage on Cloud infrastructure, run the documented echo command, execute the pipeline from a Git branch, and observe the first build complete.
assumed_prior_state: No Harness account, organization, project, entitlement, source repository, Git-provider connection, connector, pipeline, stage, build infrastructure, command step, or execution is assumed.
A browser, working identity, network connection, and access to a source-code repository are available.
No signup, email verification, SSO, repository authorization, connector creation, pipeline creation, or build execution was performed during research.

documented_first_success.official_milestone: Run your first Harness CI pipeline.
documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: A Cloud CI build for a selected Git branch executes the configured Run step containing `echo "Harness CI is fast"`.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: The pipeline execution reaches a successful terminal state and its build logs contain the Run step output `Harness CI is fast`.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: A Harness CI execution completes after the documented Run step executes `echo "Harness CI is fast"`.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: This is the earliest observable terminal in the selected official quickstart. The workshop policy permits treating signup as one opaque step and then reconstructing the publicly documented post-login path.

prerequisites.requirement: Create a Harness account or sign in to an existing account before accessing the Continuous Integration module.
Have access to a Harness organization and project in which a CI pipeline can be created.
Have a source-code repository and branch available through Harness Code Repository or a supported third-party Git provider.
Create or select a Harness connector when the source repository is hosted by a third-party Git provider.
Use an identity with permission to create and execute pipelines, select the repository, and use Cloud build infrastructure in the target project.

primary_path.action: Open the official Harness CI onboarding guide.
primary_path.output: The first-build sequence and prerequisites are visible.
primary_path.success_signal: The guide exposes Create your first CI pipeline.

primary_path.action: Sign up for Harness as one opaque account step.
primary_path.output: An authenticated Harness account is available.
primary_path.success_signal: The Harness console opens for the new account.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Complete any signup or verification requested by Harness; individual form fields are outside this record.

primary_path.action: Open the Continuous Integration module, select Pipelines, and choose Create a Pipeline.
primary_path.success_signal: Name and storage controls are visible.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The account must have organization and project access.

primary_path.action: Name the pipeline, choose Inline storage, and select Start.
primary_path.output: An inline pipeline opens in Pipeline Studio.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Remote storage would add a Git configuration dependency.

primary_path.action: Add a Build stage, select the source provider and repository, and create or select a connector if required.
primary_path.output: The Build stage codebase is configured.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Third-party repositories require a valid connector.

primary_path.action: Choose Harness Cloud build infrastructure and continue with the documented operating-system and architecture defaults.
primary_path.output: The stage has managed Cloud infrastructure.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Cloud entitlement and project permission are required.

primary_path.action: In Execution, add a Run step and enter `echo "Harness CI is fast"`, then apply and save.
primary_path.output: The pipeline contains the minimal executable step.
primary_path.success_signal: The Run step is visible in the saved stage.

primary_path.action: Select Run, choose Git Branch, enter the branch, and select Run Pipeline.
primary_path.success_signal: The execution view and build logs appear.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: An unavailable branch or connector causes the run to fail.

primary_path.action: Inspect the completed execution and Run-step logs.
primary_path.output: The first CI build is complete.
primary_path.success_signal: The execution succeeds and the logs contain `Harness CI is fast`.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Use the failed step and build logs if the execution does not succeed.

branches.path: Select an existing repository hosted by Harness when configuring the Build stage.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Avoids a third-party Git connector, but repository creation remains a prerequisite outside the CI onboarding guide.

branches.path: Create or select the required provider connector, authorize its repository access, and select the repository.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds an external authentication and repository-permission boundary before build infrastructure setup.

branches.path: Choose Remote instead of Inline and configure the repository location that will store the pipeline YAML.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds Git connector and remote-file configuration to pipeline creation.

branches.path: Select and configure an alternate infrastructure type supported by Harness CI.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds infrastructure provisioning and configuration rather than using the onboarding guide's Cloud defaults.

friction_gates.description: The CI module requires a Harness account and authenticated console session.
The public official sources do not expose the complete no-account signup and verification sequence.
The developer must have or create the correct organization and project context before pipeline creation.
A source-code repository and branch must already be accessible to the developer.
A third-party Git repository requires a Harness connector and external provider authorization.
The developer must choose Git provider, repository, pipeline storage, build infrastructure, operating system, architecture, build type, and branch.
Pipeline creation, connector use, repository selection, Cloud infrastructure, and execution depend on project permissions and available entitlements.

sources.title: CI pipeline creation overview
evidence_supported: Account or Try Harness CI entry

sources.title: Harness
evidence_supported: Absence of a server-rendered account-completion sequence

excluded_after_success: Repository creation and application code, compilation and test steps, cache and service dependencies, secrets, artifact publication, pull-request and webhook triggers, failure handling, quality gates, security scans, templates, notifications, deployments, governance, production build infrastructure, and cost optimization occur after the first minimal build.
